executive_summary: OpenAI secured a Pentagon deal for classified AI deployment after the Trump administration blacklisted rival Anthropic, marking a decisive fusion of frontier AI with national security infrastructure. Congressional fiscal brinkmanship intensified as an 18-day DHS shutdown persisted alongside Republican efforts to pass a continuing resolution, while the administration escalated election-control efforts through sweeping legislation and FBI ballot seizures. The Palantir-DHS billion-dollar blanket purchase agreement and TerraPower's historic nuclear construction permit signal accelerating techno-oligarchic entanglement and energy regime shifts.

person_updates.name: John Thune
person_updates.summary: As Senate Majority Leader, Thune led the charge against Democrats over the 18-day DHS shutdown, framing it as a partisan weapon to hinder GOP midterm campaigns. He also advanced the bipartisan ROAD to Housing bill through an 84-6 cloture vote, demonstrating continued gatekeeping power over the legislative calendar.

person_updates.name: Susan M. Collins
person_updates.summary: Collins formally announced her bid for a sixth Senate term on March 3, positioning herself as an independent moderate in a blue-leaning state. She deflected attacks from Democratic challengers while emphasizing her record of breaking with Trump on key votes, reinforcing her pivotal swing role in the Senate.

person_updates.name: Mitch McConnell
person_updates.summary: McConnell delivered floor remarks opposing the Kaine War Powers Resolution and publicly backed U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran that killed Ayatollah Khamenei, asserting traditional Republican deference to executive military authority. His confirmed decision not to seek reelection in 2026 reshapes Kentucky's political landscape and his lame-duck influence on defense and fiscal matters.

person_updates.name: Sam Altman
person_updates.summary: Altman secured a Pentagon deal to deploy OpenAI models on classified networks after Anthropic was blacklisted by the Trump administration, then publicly admitted rushing the agreement and amended it to prohibit domestic surveillance. At a Morgan Stanley conference, he argued governments must remain more powerful than private AI companies, positioning OpenAI as the cooperative partner of choice for national security AI.

person_updates.name: Jensen Huang
person_updates.summary: Huang announced GTC 2026 for March 16-19 with a teased mystery chip reveal, committed an additional $2 billion to CoreWeave for AI factory expansion, and declined to invest $100 billion in OpenAI, signaling strategic independence. His declaration that the era of massive private AI investments is ending while chip demand remains relentless positions Nvidia as the infrastructure kingmaker.

person_updates.name: Bret Taylor
person_updates.summary: At Mobile World Congress on March 5, Taylor announced that AI model costs have dropped 100-fold in 18 months while quality improved 50%, predicting customer service calls could cost near one cent. He also revealed an OpenAI board policy banning AI use for meeting preparation, establishing a governance standard that balances AI optimism with human-centric oversight.

person_updates.name: Lisa Su
person_updates.summary: AMD secured a landmark $100 billion deal with Meta for six gigawatts of AI computing power, potentially giving Meta up to 10% ownership of AMD stock. Su's CES 2026 keynote unveiling MI500 series accelerators on 2nm with a promised 1000X performance increase by 2027 directly challenges Nvidia's dominance in the AI chip market.

person_updates.name: Elon Musk
person_updates.summary: Musk testified in a San Francisco federal court fraud trial on March 4-5 defending 2022 tweets that allegedly manipulated Twitter's stock price, with investors seeking up to $1 billion in damages. No significant developments emerged in his DOGE role or other key influence areas, though courts have continued blocking some of his prior agency cuts and contract terminations.

person_updates.name: John Malone
person_updates.summary: Malone continued his transition to chairman emeritus roles at Liberty Global and Liberty Media, with Apple's exclusive U.S. Formula One television rights deal representing a significant monetization event for one of his most valuable assets. No new structural moves emerged during the reporting period.

person_updates.name: Donald Trump
person_updates.summary: Trump fired DHS Secretary Kristi Noem and nominated Sen. Markwayne Mullin as replacement, declared Operation Epic Fury against Iran 'far ahead of schedule,' and saw mixed results from Texas primary endorsements. The DOJ released Epstein files containing unsubstantiated allegations against him, which the White House dismissed, while his administration continued pushing federal election control measures.

person_updates.name: Alex Karp
person_updates.summary: Palantir reported 70% year-over-year revenue growth to $1.41 billion with a $448 million Navy contract, while DHS awarded the company a $1 billion blanket purchase agreement for Gotham and Foundry platforms without competitive bidding. At the a16z American Dynamism Summit, Karp warned Silicon Valley CEOs that opposing military AI ties would lead to government nationalization of their technology.

person_updates.name: Ron DeSantis
person_updates.summary: DeSantis endorsed SB 1756 expanding vaccine exemptions and ivermectin access, while a federal judge blocked his executive order designating CAIR and the Muslim Brotherhood as terrorist organizations on First Amendment grounds. He also made four new judicial appointments to Florida courts, advancing his long-term reshaping of the state judiciary.

person_updates.name: Chuck Grassley
person_updates.summary: Grassley expressed cautious support for a bipartisan health care proposal while insisting on income eligibility caps and anti-fraud measures, signaling a potential Republican shift toward extending ACA subsidies after the 43-day government shutdown. His meeting with Turning Point USA leadership and advocacy for cameras in federal courts reinforced his dual role as fiscal gatekeeper and judiciary reformer.

person_updates.name: Cynthia M. Lummis
person_updates.summary: Lummis had no significant developments this week, having previously announced in December 2025 that she will not seek reelection in 2026. Her departure diminishes direct influence on crypto regulation and energy policy in the Senate.

person_updates.name: Brett M. Kavanaugh
person_updates.summary: Kavanaugh joined a split emergency ruling siding with parents challenging California transgender disclosure policies on Free Exercise and Due Process grounds, and dissented in a tariff refunds case criticizing broad judicial remedies as a 'mess.' His positions consistently reinforced executive power deference and religious liberty prioritization over state administrative mandates.

person_updates.name: Russell M. Nelson
person_updates.summary: No developments this week. Nelson passed away on September 27, 2025, and leadership of the Church has transitioned to Dallin H. Oaks.

person_updates.name: Gavin Newsom
person_updates.summary: Newsom announced $291 million in homelessness and mental health funding with new CARE Court accountability measures, and appeared on Jimmy Kimmel Live accusing Trump of federalizing the National Guard and deploying military assets to suppress voting. He predicted Trump would be 'crushed' in the midterms, positioning himself as a leading Democratic counter-mobilization figure and potential 2028 contender.

person_updates.name: Ken Langone
person_updates.summary: No significant hard news about Ken Langone emerged during the reporting period. Available results cover only outdated events and past philanthropy.

person_updates.name: Abigail Johnson
person_updates.summary: No significant developments for Abigail Johnson were identified during the reporting period. Her net worth remains tied to Fidelity's crypto expansions but no new actions or statements surfaced.

person_updates.name: Alice Walton
person_updates.summary: No significant hard news about Alice Walton emerged during the reporting period. She remains listed as America's richest woman at $106 billion net worth.

person_updates.name: Pam Bondi
person_updates.summary: Bondi was subpoenaed by the House Oversight Committee to testify on DOJ handling of Epstein files, signed a draft rule limiting state ethics probes into DOJ lawyers, and rescinded a ban on political appointees attending campaign events. These moves collectively expand executive insulation while drawing bipartisan congressional scrutiny.

person_updates.name: J.B. Pritzker
person_updates.summary: Pritzker filed petitions for a third gubernatorial term while demanding federal funds withheld by the Trump administration, touting Illinois's 10 credit upgrades and a record $48.5 billion budget. He announced $1 billion in medical debt elimination for 520,000 Illinoisans and pushed nuclear energy revival by lifting the state's moratorium, positioning himself as a national Democratic counter-mobilization leader.

person_updates.name: Lukas Walton
person_updates.summary: No significant developments for Lukas Walton were identified during the reporting period beyond a routine Forbes ranking listing him as Illinois's richest person at $39 billion.

person_updates.name: Pierre Omidyar
person_updates.summary: No significant hard news about Pierre Omidyar emerged during the reporting period. The only proximate event was an eBay stalking scandal civil suit settlement on February 25, which falls just outside the reporting window.

person_updates.name: Henry Kravis
person_updates.summary: KKR sold nearly 20 million shares of BrightSpring Health Services at $40.96 per share, while Kravis publicly warned against politicizing the Federal Reserve, citing Turkey as a cautionary example. He also committed $20 billion in India investments over the next decade, expanding KKR's global infrastructure footprint.

person_updates.name: Tom Cotton
person_updates.summary: Cotton won the Arkansas Republican primary with 82% of the vote and immediately launched his general election campaign, while appearing on Fox News to praise U.S. surgical strikes eliminating Iranian leaders. His dominant primary victory and hawkish posture reinforce his position as a leading Republican voice on defense and intelligence matters.

person_updates.name: Jeremy Jacobs, Sr. & family
person_updates.summary: No significant hard news about Jeremy Jacobs Sr. or family emerged during the reporting period. Available results reference only older profiles and routine hockey governance activities.

phase_sequence_updates.name: Executive Power Consolidation
phase_sequence_updates.summary: The week featured Trump's firing of DHS Secretary Noem and Bondi's moves to insulate DOJ lawyers from state ethics probes and free political appointees for campaign activities, but no major structural expansions of presidential authority through new executive orders or DOGE-style restructurings.

phase_sequence_updates.name: Judicial Deconstruction of the Administrative State
phase_sequence_updates.summary: Kavanaugh's emergency ruling on parental rights and his tariff dissent reinforced conservative judicial priorities, but no landmark administrative law rulings or cert grants emerged this week. A federal judge blocking DeSantis's terrorist designation order demonstrated continued judicial pushback on executive overreach at the state level.

phase_sequence_updates.name: Congressional Gatekeeping & Fiscal Brinkmanship
phase_sequence_updates.summary: The 18-day DHS shutdown persisted as Republicans finalized a continuing resolution extending FY2025 funding through November 21, while Democrats demanded ACA subsidy extensions and bipartisan health care talks stalled. Senate appropriators advanced a three-bill minibus but broader FY2026 negotiations remain tense amid fiscal conservative pushes for flat funding.

phase_sequence_updates.name: AI Compute & Model Supremacy
phase_sequence_updates.summary: OpenAI's Pentagon classified-network deal, AMD's $100 billion Meta partnership, Nvidia's $2 billion CoreWeave commitment, and Bret Taylor's announcement of 100-fold cost reductions collectively signal an intensifying race across the full AI stack. The Anthropic blacklisting by the Trump administration introduced a new dimension of government-directed market shaping in frontier AI.

phase_sequence_updates.name: Monetary-Fiscal-Financial Regime Tension
phase_sequence_updates.summary: No rate decisions, bond auctions, or major regulatory actions occurred this week, with the Fed holding at 3.5-3.75% from its January meeting. Henry Kravis's public warning against politicizing the Federal Reserve was the most notable signal of ongoing structural tension between executive fiscal ambitions and central bank independence.

phase_sequence_updates.name: Defense Industrial Base Realignment
phase_sequence_updates.summary: The House Armed Services Committee held a 'Speed to Scale' hearing on revitalizing the defense industrial base, while Phase 2 of the Revolutionary FAR Overhaul solicited stakeholder input to streamline acquisition. OpenAI's Pentagon deal and Palantir's $1 billion DHS agreement signal accelerating integration of software-defined, AI-centric firms into the national security apparatus.

phase_sequence_updates.name: Algorithmic Narrative Dominance
phase_sequence_updates.summary: No major platform policy changes, deplatformings, or regulatory pressures emerged this week. Musk's fraud trial over Twitter stock manipulation tweets was the most notable event, testing accountability for platform-owner narrative control.

phase_sequence_updates.name: Energy Permitting & Regime Competition
phase_sequence_updates.summary: The NRC approved TerraPower's construction permit for the Kemmerer Unit 1 advanced nuclear reactor on March 4—the first commercial nuclear construction permit in years—signaling meaningful nuclear revival. Bipartisan permitting reform calls intensified with a coalition letter to the Senate, while Illinois lifted its nuclear moratorium under Pritzker's leadership.

phase_sequence_updates.name: Techno-Oligarchic Entanglement
phase_sequence_updates.summary: DHS awarded Palantir a $1 billion blanket purchase agreement without competitive bidding, while OpenAI's Pentagon deal followed the administration's blacklisting of its competitor Anthropic. Karp's warning that Silicon Valley firms opposing military ties face nationalization and OpenAI's quadrupled lobbying spend underscore deepening government-tech fusion.

phase_sequence_updates.name: Election Legitimacy & Democratic Counter-Mobilization
phase_sequence_updates.summary: Congressional Republicans introduced two sweeping election bills allowing the AG and private citizens to sue election officials, while the FBI seized 2020 Fulton County ballots and Trump called for federal takeover of state elections. Courts struck down key provisions of Trump's election executive order, establishing a structural conflict between centralization efforts and judicial constraints.

top_stories.headline: OpenAI Secures Pentagon Classified Network Deal After Trump Administration Blacklists Anthropic
top_stories.significance: The administration's exclusion of Anthropic and simultaneous OpenAI deal establishes a government-directed winner in frontier AI for national security, fusing executive power with corporate AI supremacy.

top_stories.headline: Palantir Awarded $1 Billion DHS Blanket Purchase Agreement Without Competitive Bidding
top_stories.significance: The no-bid agreement across CBP, ICE, FEMA, and CISA deepens the structural dependency of federal law enforcement and homeland security on a single tech firm aligned with the governing coalition.

top_stories.headline: DHS Shutdown Enters 18th Day as Republicans Push Continuing Resolution and Democrats Demand ACA Extensions
top_stories.significance: The prolonged shutdown and competing fiscal demands test Republican unity on spending while Democrats leverage health care subsidies as a wedge issue ahead of midterms.

top_stories.headline: NRC Approves TerraPower's First Commercial Nuclear Construction Permit in Years
top_stories.significance: The Kemmerer Unit 1 permit signals a genuine inflection point in nuclear revival driven by soaring AI and electrification demand, with implications for the entire energy-compute supply chain.

top_stories.headline: GOP Election Bills Would Let AG and Private Citizens Sue Election Officials; FBI Seizes Fulton County Ballots
top_stories.significance: The convergence of legislative, executive, and investigative actions to federalize election administration represents the most aggressive structural challenge to state-run elections in modern history.

edge_signals.evidence: The Trump administration blacklisted Anthropic as a supply-chain risk hours before OpenAI announced its Pentagon classified-network deployment deal, with Altman subsequently stating governments must remain more powerful than private AI companies.
DHS awarded Palantir a five-year $1 billion blanket purchase agreement for Gotham and Foundry platforms across CBP, ICE, FEMA, and CISA without competitive bidding under the Trump administration.

Bondi rescinded DOJ restrictions on political appointees attending campaign events and proposed rules shielding DOJ lawyers from state ethics probes while the administration pushed sweeping federal election control legislation.

Both senior Iowa-connected Republican senators aligned on fiscal brinkmanship strategy, with Thune managing the DHS shutdown floor fight while Grassley signaled potential openness to ACA subsidy extensions with conditions.
